stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • One of the most influential medieval libraries was the Library of Toledo in Toledo, Spain.
  • It was founded in the twelfth century with the goal of preserving and transmitting scholarly knowledge.
  • Scholars at the Library of Toledo gathered Arabic and Greek manuscripts from across the Mediterranean and translated them into Latin.
  • Translations included works by the Greek physician Galen and the Persian polymath Avicenna.
  • The library's translation school produced thousands of Latin parchment copies that circulated throughout Europe.
stem: The student wants to explain how the Library of Toledo preserved scholarly knowledge. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The Library of Toledo was known for gathering knowledge from across the Mediterranean, including from the Arab and Greek worlds.
Founded in Spain in the twelfth century, the Library of Toledo employed many scholars as translators.
Writings from the Greek physician Galen and the Persian polymath Avicenna were preserved at the Library of Toledo.
The Library of Toledo gathered Arabic and Greek manuscripts and produced thousands of Latin parchment copies that could be studied and circulated.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer because it directly explains how the library preserved knowledge: by gathering source manuscripts and producing Latin copies that could be studied and shared.

Choices A, B, and C give related facts but don't explain the preservation mechanism.
